data.title: Trump cautions against tariff repeal, citing billions collected. White House could lose if court rules against.
data.description: The president has expressed satisfaction with the significant amount of money, amounting to billions of dollars, gathered through tariffs. However, this revenue could face jeopardy if the White House does not prevail in the ongoing legal dispute.

data.title: Susan G. Komen® to host MORE THAN PINK Walk in Fort Worth aiming to raise $565.5K for breast cancer organization.
data.description: In Fort Worth, Texas, Susan G. Komen®, the top breast cancer organization globally, will host the 2025 Fort Worth MORE THAN PINK Walk, bringing together breast cancer survivors, individuals with metastatic breast cancer (MBC), and supporters. The event aims to raise $565.5K for Komen's mission. This fundraising initiative is crucial for Komen to sustain its vital work in saving lives through research and support. The walk serves as a platform to unite the community and raise awareness about breast cancer.

data.title: Blondie's 1999 album "No Exit" is being reissued in a Deluxe Edition on October 31 via BMG, marking their 17-year comeback.
data.description: In New York, Blondie’s No Exit, their comeback album from 1999 following a 17-year break, is set to be reissued as a Deluxe Edition by BMG on October 31. This definitive release of one of the band’s key records will be newly remastered and expanded, marking a significant point in Blondie’s career. For the first time, the album will also be released on vinyl alongside other formats. This reissue commemorates Blondie’s impressive return to the music scene and reaffirms their status as a legendary band.

data.title: US and Japan are reportedly close to a deal to reduce tariffs on Japanese car imports, dropping from 27.5% to 15% soon, taking effect by the end of September after a U.S. presidential executive order. Trump's approval is necessary.
data.description: The United States and Japan are close to finalizing an agreement to decrease tariffs on Japanese car imports. The deal is anticipated to be formalized within two weeks of a U.S. presidential executive order. A Japanese government source disclosed to Reuters that the U.S. and Japan are in the concluding stages of talks to lower tariffs on imported Japanese vehicles. The updated tariff rate will be 15%, a significant decrease from the existing 27.5%. The adjusted tariff is projected to take effect by the end of September, with the exact date to be specified in the forthcoming U.S. presidential executive order. The ultimate decision lies with U.S. President Donald Trump. In July, the U.S. approved lowering tariffs on Japanese auto imports, but the timing remains uncertain pending Trump's executive order signature. Japan's chief trade negotiator, Ryosei Akazawa, also mentioned in the report...

data.title: Toro Company's fiscal Q3 of 2025 shows growth in underground construction and golf/grounds sectors.
data.description: In Bloomington, Minnesota, The Toro Company has released its financial results for the third quarter of Fiscal 2025. The company saw notable growth, particularly in the underground construction and golf and grounds sectors. This positive performance has contributed to the company's overall success during this period.

data.title: MIPS appoints Michael Zirngibl as VP Business Development for EMEA to accelerate growth. Important details: region focus, role, sector experience.
data.description: MIPS, a company under GlobalFoundries, has welcomed Michael Zirngibl as their new Vice President of Business Development for EMEA. With a focus on expanding business operations in the region, Michael brings his expertise in automotive, industrial, and communications infrastructure sectors to drive MIPS' mission of advancing physical AI. This strategic move underlines the potential for MIPS technology in this field and signals a new phase of growth for the company.

data.title: Cenlar names Mike Merwin Senior VP of Investment Management, will introduce new bank offerings for clients.
data.description: Ewing, New Jersey - Cenlar, a prominent commercial bank and top mortgage loan subservicer, has appointed Mike Merwin as the Senior Vice President of Investment Management. In this role, Merwin will be instrumental in aiding Cenlar in utilizing its extensive mortgage knowledge and banking framework to introduce enhanced services for clients. These new offerings by the bank, overseen by Cenlar's Chief Banking and Capital Markets Officer, JB Long, encompass a loan acquisition conduit and warehousing solutions.
